evidence: Multiple crate roots repeat templated narrative/comment blocks and the same boilerplate re-export framing (e.g., `crates/temper-agent-runtime/src/lib.rs`, `crates/temper-codegen/src/lib.rs`, `crates/temper-jit/src/lib.rs`, `crates/temper-odata/src/lib.rs`, `crates/temper-observe/src/lib.rs`).

Very small pass-through facade files exist with no behavior and only re-exports (e.g., `crates/temper-cli/src/util.rs`, `crates/temper-mcp/src/repl.rs`), which increases surface area without adding domain logic.
ai_generated_debt.issues_preventing_higher_score: Template-heavy comments and zero-logic facade modules add low-value maintenance noise even though they are not severe defects.

evidence: `crates/temper-evolution/src/lib.rs:27-28` suppresses deprecation and still re-exports `store::RecordStore` via `#[allow(deprecated)]`, indicating a legacy API path remains active instead of being fully retired or isolated.

`crates/temper-mcp/src/error.rs` claims migration from `anyhow::Error`/bare strings to typed errors, but most variants still store `String` and `From<...>` impls convert rich errors to text (`err.to_string()`), losing typed context.
`crates/temper-agent-runtime/src/tools/mod.rs:11,55` still uses `anyhow::Result<ToolResult>`, showing mixed old/new error contracts across neighboring crates rather than a completed migration.

README.md asserts hard governance guarantees like "Every spec passes four levels before reaching production. No exceptions." (README.md:111), but assigned runtime boundary modules have limited contract-level tests.
crates/temper-server/src/events.rs implements `/tdata/$events` stream behavior (events.rs:42-58), while its test coverage only checks struct serialization (events.rs:61-82).

crates/temper-server/src/observe/evolution/records_list.rs has filtering and response-shaping logic (records_list.rs:19-128), but observe tests only cover empty/basic scenarios (observe/mod_test.rs:745-817,1201-1222) and not query-filter or payload-shape edge cases.

crates/temper-cli/src/serve/storage.rs contains startup persistence/recovery paths (storage.rs:39-369,472-499) that are invoked by serve startup (serve/mod.rs:78,132,137,147,149), but local tests only exercise URL redaction and a status helper (storage.rs:371-470).
Optimization API uses abbreviated type names (`OptCategory`, `OptAction`) while most other crates use fully spelled domain nouns (`RecordType`, `ObserveError`, `AuthzError`).
Public evolution/observe/server APIs are generally explicit, but abbreviation islands increase vocabulary switching cost.
naming_quality.issues_preventing_higher_score: Abbreviated public type names in `temper-optimize` diverge from the more explicit naming style used across sibling crates.

evidence: `generate_insights` marks unmet intent as resolved if any `SubmitSpec` exists for an entity type, without checking event ordering (`crates/temper-server/src/insight_generator.rs:112-119`).

`generate_unmet_intents` uses the same entity-level presence check (`submitted_specs.contains_key`) and can mark failures as resolved even when failures occur after submit (`crates/temper-server/src/insight_generator.rs:307-309`).

Evolution filtering takes free-form `Option<String>` for `record_type`/`status` and forwards them directly (`crates/temper-server/src/observe/evolution/records_list.rs:12-27`) instead of using `RecordType`/`RecordStatus` enums from `temper-evolution`.
Several response payloads are built as untyped `serde_json::Value` maps, reducing compile-time guarantees on API field stability.
type_safety.issues_preventing_higher_score: Stringly-typed request filters and untyped response assembly leave API-level typing weaker than the internal enum model.

evidence: `run(name)` is documented as `temper init <name>`, but writes the raw input into package `name` (`crates/temper-cli/src/init/mod.rs:43-86`), so path-like input can generate invalid Cargo package names.
Tests encode this behavior by asserting `Cargo.toml` contains the full path string (`crates/temper-cli/src/init/mod.rs:174-177`), which conflicts with Cargo package naming constraints.
SSE event serialization failure is silently converted to empty payload via `unwrap_or_default()` (`crates/temper-server/src/events.rs:50`).

`/observe/evolution` handlers log Turso errors and return successful empty responses instead of surfacing transport/query failures (`crates/temper-server/src/observe/evolution/records_list.rs:67-80`, `117-127`).
No import-time network or DB side effects were found in the reviewed files; Postgres/Turso connections are established in explicit async functions (`crates/temper-cli/src/serve/storage.rs:39-59`).
Module roots are mostly re-export-only and avoid startup-order-sensitive globals (`crates/temper-agent-runtime/src/lib.rs`, `crates/temper-observe/src/lib.rs`, `crates/temper-odata/src/lib.rs`).
initialization_coupling.issues_preventing_higher_score: Bootstrap still couples runtime restoration and DB migration into single connect paths, which can complicate isolated startup testing.

evidence: Error enums are mostly crate-specific and coherent, but naming convention drifts exist (`ProviderError`/`ConnectionError` vs short `Tool`/`Runtime` variants across crates).
Public APIs mix strongly typed domain enums in core crates with ad-hoc string status fields in server DTOs.
convention_outlier.issues_preventing_higher_score: Conventions are mostly consistent, but server-facing DTO conventions drift toward stringly contracts compared with enum-heavy core crates.

evidence: Evolution API accepts unconstrained string filters while canonical enums already exist (`crates/temper-server/src/observe/evolution/records_list.rs:12-27`, `crates/temper-evolution/src/records.rs:16-29`, `64-73`).
Handler responses merge arbitrary JSON `data` fields into top-level objects (`records_list.rs:43-49`, `100-107`), making field shape collision-prone and less predictable.

`load_registry_from_postgres` and `load_registry_from_turso` duplicate near-identical grouping/restore logic with only storage access differences (`crates/temper-cli/src/serve/storage.rs:212-294` vs `296-369`).
Evolution list/insight handlers duplicate JSON merge and fallback behavior patterns (`crates/temper-server/src/observe/evolution/records_list.rs:29-80` and `92-127`).

findings.summary: Deprecated evolution store API is still exported behind allow(deprecated)
evidence: `crates/temper-evolution/src/lib.rs:27-28` contains `#[allow(deprecated)] pub use store::RecordStore;`, keeping deprecated API reachable from crate root.
`records.rs` defines the current record model and is already the active domain surface, so continued root export of deprecated store trait keeps both old and new paths alive.
findings.suggestion: Complete the migration by removing the deprecated root re-export, or move it behind an explicit `legacy` feature/module with a removal timeline. Update downstream imports to non-deprecated store interfaces before deleting the shim.

findings.summary: Error-type migration is partial and still stringly/anyhow at subsystem boundaries
evidence: `crates/temper-mcp/src/error.rs:3-6` states the crate moved to a unified typed `McpError`, but variants like `Transport(String)`, `Tool(String)`, `Runtime(String)`, `Config(String)` and `From` impls (`err.to_string()`) collapse source error types.
`crates/temper-agent-runtime/src/tools/mod.rs:11,55` still exposes `anyhow::Result<ToolResult>` in a core trait, which preserves generic-error flow in adjacent runtime code.
`crates/temper-observe/src/error.rs` mixes one typed `#[from] serde_json::Error` variant with multiple `String` variants, reflecting an unfinished conversion to structured error payloads.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on crate-specific typed errors across runtime/MCP/observe boundaries: replace string payload variants with structured variants carrying source errors (`#[from]` where possible), and migrate `ToolRegistry::execute` away from `anyhow::Result` to a dedicated runtime error enum.

findings.summary: Documented governance guarantees are not backed by executable contract tests in this batch.
evidence: README states absolute guarantees (e.g., "No exceptions" for verification cascade at README.md:111), while tested coverage in assigned files is mostly unit-level/helper-level.
Critical runtime boundary functions in events/records_list/storage lack end-to-end assertions that would fail on contract regressions.
findings.suggestion: Add a governance contract test suite that maps README-level guarantees to executable checks across storage recovery, event streaming, and evolution API behavior; enforce it in CI as a quality gate.

findings.summary: The `/tdata/$events` SSE handler lacks route-level tests for stream semantics.
evidence: `handle_events` performs runtime stream mapping and drops lagged broadcast errors (events.rs:43-55), but tests in this module only verify JSON serialization of `EntityStateChange` (events.rs:61-82).
Existing SSE test coverage in observe tests targets a different endpoint (`/api/tenants/default/decisions/stream`) rather than `/tdata/$events` (observe/mod_test.rs:162-184).
findings.suggestion: Add async integration tests that mount the router with `/tdata/$events`, publish broadcast messages, and assert `text/event-stream`, event name `state_change`, payload content, and lagged-receiver behavior.

findings.summary: Evolution list endpoints are not tested for filter and payload-shaping contracts.
evidence: `list_evolution_records` accepts `record_type`/`status` query filters and merges `r.data` fields into top-level response objects (records_list.rs:10-64), but tests only cover empty/basic totals (observe/mod_test.rs:745-817).
`list_evolution_insights` merges JSON data into each insight item (records_list.rs:85-115), while only empty response behavior is tested (observe/mod_test.rs:1201-1222).
findings.suggestion: Add table-driven endpoint tests that seed Turso rows and assert query filtering, stable response keys, and deterministic behavior when `data` includes conflicting keys or malformed JSON.

findings.summary: Startup persistence and registry-restore paths are effectively unvalidated by integration tests.
evidence: Storage module includes connection/migration/upsert/load flows for Postgres and Turso (storage.rs:39-59,77-127,212-369,472-499), but tests in that file only cover URL redaction and `row_to_registry_status` helper logic (storage.rs:371-470).
These storage functions are on the `serve::run` startup path that controls persisted-spec recovery and loading (serve/mod.rs:78,132,137,147,149).
findings.suggestion: Add backend integration tests (Postgres test DB + Turso file DB) that run upsert/load round-trips, validate restored verification states and tenant constraints, and assert startup behavior under malformed or missing persisted records.

findings.summary: Unmet-intent resolution logic ignores timestamp ordering and can mark active failures as resolved
evidence: `generate_insights` sets `resolved` based on entity presence in `submitted_types`, not whether submit happened after failures (`insight_generator.rs:112-119`).
`generate_unmet_intents` uses `submitted_specs.contains_key` similarly (`insight_generator.rs:307-309`), so later failures can still be labeled resolved in records served by evolution endpoints.
findings.suggestion: Track submit timestamps per entity and resolve only when `submit_timestamp >= last_failure_timestamp` for each grouped signal/intent; keep groups open when failures continue after submit.

findings.summary: SSE stream silently emits empty data when event serialization fails
evidence: `serde_json::to_string(&change).unwrap_or_default()` replaces serialization failure with empty string (`events.rs:50`).
Elsewhere (e.g., MCP) serialization issues are typed and surfaced (`McpError::Serialization`, `crates/temper-mcp/src/error.rs:52-55`), creating inconsistent failure behavior.
findings.suggestion: Replace `unwrap_or_default` with explicit error handling: log the serialization error with entity context and either skip the event or emit a structured error event, but never substitute silent empty payloads.

findings.summary: Evolution endpoints return 200+empty payload when Turso query fails
evidence: On Turso query errors, handlers only `warn!` and then return empty arrays (`records_list.rs:67-80`, `117-127`).
Storage layer otherwise treats DB failures as hard errors (`anyhow` propagation in `storage.rs:216-233`, `301-308`), so API behavior diverges from backend error contract.
findings.suggestion: Return explicit error responses (e.g., 503/500 with stable error envelope) when persistent-store queries fail; keep empty payload fallback only for the explicit "store not configured" branch.

findings.summary: `temper init` treats path-like input as Cargo package name, violating Cargo naming contract
evidence: `run(name)` writes `name` directly into `Cargo.toml` package name template (`init/mod.rs:34-37`, `84-86`) without sanitization.
Test passes absolute path as input and asserts that path appears in package name (`init/mod.rs:148-150`, `174-177`), locking in invalid-manifest behavior for non-simple names.
findings.suggestion: Derive package name from `Path::file_name()` and normalize to Cargo-valid `[a-zA-Z0-9_-]` format, while still using full path for destination directory.

findings.summary: Evolution API filters are free-form strings despite canonical enum types
evidence: `EvolutionRecordParams` uses `Option<String>` for `record_type` and `status` (`records_list.rs:10-15`) and forwards directly to storage (`:26-27`).
Canonical enums already define valid values (`RecordType`, `RecordStatus` in `records.rs:16-29`, `64-73`), but endpoint contract does not enforce them.
findings.suggestion: Use typed query DTOs backed by enum deserialization (`serde(rename_all = "lowercase")`) and return 400 for invalid values; keep storage signatures aligned to typed enums.

findings.summary: Postgres and Turso registry restore flows duplicate large orchestration blocks
evidence: `load_registry_from_postgres` and `load_registry_from_turso` repeat the same grouping/parsing/registration/status-restore logic with backend-specific row types (`storage.rs:212-294` and `296-369`).
The same file already introduced `SpecRowLike` abstraction (`storage.rs:130-164`) but orchestration remains duplicated, indicating partial abstraction.
Records handlers also duplicate JSON assembly/fallback blocks (`records_list.rs:29-80` and `92-127`), showing repeated endpoint orchestration patterns.
findings.suggestion: Extract backend-agnostic restore pipeline into shared helper(s) operating over a row trait plus backend loader closures; similarly extract shared evolution response assembly utilities.
